feat: 30+ reactive hooks, router crate, comprehensive API docs

New reactive utilities (oxide-core):
  - watch(source, callback) — react to signal changes, skips initial
  - on_mount(f) — run after current microtask completes
  - on_cleanup(f) — register cleanup for beforeunload

New reactive hooks (oxide-dom):
  - use_local_storage(key, default) — persistent signal
  - use_interval(ms) — reactive timer tick
  - use_debounce(signal, ms) — debounced output signal
  - use_throttle(signal, ms) — throttled output signal
  - use_window_size() — reactive (width, height)
  - use_scroll() — reactive document scroll position
  - use_mouse() — reactive global mouse position
  - use_media_query(query) — reactive CSS media query
  - use_online() — network online/offline status
  - use_preferred_dark() — prefers-color-scheme signal
  - use_click_outside(el, handler) — click outside detection
  - use_focus(el) — focus tracking signal
  - set_title(title) — set document title
  - set_meta(name, content) — set/create meta tags

New crate: oxide-router — client-side routing:
  - Router::new(mode, routes) — hash or history mode
  - route(path, view) — define routes with :param segments
  - router.view() — reactive outlet that swaps views
  - navigate(path) — programmatic navigation
  - link(href, text) / nav_link() — client-side links
  - use_route() — current path signal
  - use_param(name) / use_params() — route parameters

Docs rewrite (docs.html):
  - 16 categorized sections with table of contents
  - ~90 APIs documented with code examples
  - Categories: Getting Started, CLI, Core Reactivity, View Macro,
    Reactive Hooks, DOM Utils, Head Management, Routing, Timers,
    Storage, Console, Location, OTel, Resiliency, Components, Debugging

Framework now has 9 crates, ~90 public APIs.

Replace global @testing-library/react mock with explicit renderWithQueryClient helper (#2008)

The global `vi.mock("`@testing-library/react`")` in `vitest.setup.ts`
silently wraps every `render()` call with a new `QueryClientProvider`,
altering core testing API semantics suite-wide. This creates remount
artifacts on rerender (documented workaround in
`PipelineDetailPanel.test.tsx`) and hidden coupling in all tests.

### Changes

- **Removed global mock** from `vitest.setup.ts` — `render` now has
standard RTL semantics
- **Added `renderWithQueryClient` helper** in `tests/helpers/` using
RTL's native `wrapper` option, so `rerender()` reuses the same provider
instance
- **Migrated 18 component test files** from implicit global wrapping to
explicit helper usage
- **Simplified `DiscoverPage.test.tsx`** — replaced manual
`QueryClient`/`QueryClientProvider` setup with the shared helper
- **Updated `PipelineDetailPanel.test.tsx`** — removed stale comment
referencing the mock workaround

### Usage

```tsx
// Before: render() silently wrapped by global mock — no QueryClient visible at call site
render();

// After: explicit, uses RTL wrapper option (rerender-safe)
import { renderWithQueryClient } from "../helpers/renderWithQueryClient";
renderWithQueryClient();
```

Tests that don't need React Query use plain `render()` from
`@testing-library/react` unchanged.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1996

Decompose usePageFiltersStore into domain-scoped stores with reset registry (#2009)

`usePageFiltersStore` merged five unrelated domain slices (Fleet,
Profiling, Services, Kubernetes, Hosts) into one Zustand singleton,
making it a parallel-change hotspot where PRs touching different domains
collide in the same file. The same coupling extended to
`storeResetters.ts`, where per-domain resets were hard-coded.

### Domain-scoped stores

Split into five independent stores, each owning its own state, actions,
and types:

- `useFleetFiltersStore` — tabs, auto-refresh, agent filters
- `useProfilingFiltersStore` — filters, raw query, view mode, stacktrace
IDs
- `useServiceFiltersStore` — inventory filters, search session
- `useKubernetesFiltersStore` — cluster/namespace/tab filters
- `useHostsFiltersStore` — OS filter, search, time range

### Registration-based reset composition

New `resetRegistry.ts` — stores self-register at import time:

```ts
// in useFleetFiltersStore.ts
registerResetter("fleet", () => useFleetFiltersStore.getState().resetFleetFilters());
```

`storeResetters.ts` now consumes `getRegisteredResetters()` instead of
hard-coding domain references. Adding a new domain store no longer
requires editing the central reset list.

### Consumer migration

All 11 source consumers and 9 test files were migrated to import from
their domain store directly. `usePageFiltersStore.ts` is retained as a
backward-compat barrel that re-exports only shared types
(`ProfilingViewMode`, `FleetViewTab`, `AgentFilter`).

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1991

feat(logs): add time picker, tile count preview, and hide empty dimension tiles (#1982)

## Summary

Addresses UX issues from the merged dimension-first logs landing page
(#1971):

- **Time picker on /logs**: The `/logs` route now shows the global time
range picker so tile counts and drill-downs reflect the selected time
window.
- **Tile count preview**: Each dimension tile (Services, Hosts,
Processes, Files) shows the number of logs in the current time range
(e.g. "3,456 logs"). The tile is hidden if the field has no data in the
selected time window, meaning tiles for fields like `process.name` that
don't exist in the user's data are automatically suppressed.
- **Time-aware dimension list**: The ranked list in
`LogsDimensionListPage` is now filtered to the selected time range, so
counts match what the time picker shows.
- **Time-aware drill-down**: Clicking a value now opens Query Lab with
the time range filter baked into the query, preventing the mismatch
where tile counts were all-time but the Query Lab query showed 0
results.

## Changes

- `manifest.ts` — `showTimeControls: true` on the `/logs` route
- `logsQueryBuilder.ts` — new `timeRangeToEsqlFilter()` helper (converts
`TimeRange { from, to }` to ES|QL `@timestamp` filter)
- `useLogsTileCounts.ts` — new hook that fetches log count per dimension
in the current time range
- `LogsLandingPage.tsx` — reads time range from store, uses tile counts
to show/hide tiles and display count subtext
- `LogsDimensionListPage.tsx` — applies time range to the dimension
query and drill-down query

## Test plan

- [ ] Navigate to Logs — time picker shows in header
- [ ] Change time range — tile counts update; tiles with no data
disappear
- [ ] Click a host — Query Lab opens with time range filter in query
- [ ] Host with no recent logs shows no tile in the selected time window

feat(system): Task Manager, ILM, and Templates troubleshooting pages (#1938)

Adds three read-only troubleshooting pages under System nav for
high-friction operational workflows that previously required raw API
calls.

### New pages

- **Task Manager** (`/cluster-tasks`) — KPI cards (running, cancellable,
long-running >60s, oldest), searchable/sortable table by
action/node/runtime, detail flyover with raw JSON
- **Index Lifecycle Management** (`/ilm`) — KPI cards (managed indices,
error count, policy count, phase distribution), dual-tab
indices/policies tables with error-first default sort, detail flyover
surfacing `failed_step` + `step_info.reason`
- **Index Templates** (`/templates`) — KPI cards (index/component
counts, data-stream enabled, high-priority ≥500), dual-tab
index/component template tables, detail flyover showing composition
order and simulated output

### Implementation

- **Types**: `taskTypes.ts`, `ilmTypes.ts`, `templateTypes.ts` —
flattened row types for table rendering alongside raw API response
shapes
- **Client methods**: `listTasks`, `cancelTask`, `getIlmPolicies`,
`getIlmExplain`, `getIndexTemplates`, `getComponentTemplates`,
`simulateIndexTemplate`
- **Hooks**: `useTasks`, `useIlm`, `useTemplates`,
`useSimulatedIndexTemplate` — standard `useQuery` + `DataFetchResult`
pattern; `useIlm` and `useTemplates` use `Promise.all` for parallel
fetches
- **Manifest**: Tasks promoted from hidden to sidebar-visible; ILM
(order 31) and Templates (order 22) added under System
- **Ingest Pipelines**: pipeline detail panel now includes expandable
processor hotspots with matched processor config details
- **Mocks**: Full mock data + route handlers in
`elasticsearch-mocks.mjs` for all new endpoints
- **Tests**: 39 unit tests (sorting, filtering, metric derivation), E2E
smoke test for route/render, AppSidebar test updated

All pages follow existing conventions: `LinearProgress` (not
`CircularProgress`), `COMPONENT_HEIGHTS` tokens, right-side `Drawer`
flyover, `EmptyState` for zero results, `PageHeader` +
`OverviewInfoCard` layout.

feat: first-class OpenTelemetry and resiliency framework

New crates:

oxide-telemetry — OpenTelemetry-compatible tracing for WASM apps:
  - Automatic instrumentation via runtime hooks (signal lifecycle,
    effect execution, batch operations)
  - W3C trace context propagation (traceparent header on fetch)
  - traced_fetch() for distributed tracing across frontend/backend
  - Span model with attributes, duration, trace/span IDs
  - In-memory span buffer with stats (signals created/read/written,
    effects run, total effect time)
  - RAII SpanGuard for manual instrumentation
  - Zero overhead when not initialized (Cell-based fn pointer check)

oxide-resiliency — Production resiliency patterns:
  - error_boundary() — catch panics, render fallback UI
  - default_error_boundary() — styled error card
  - retry() — async retry with exponential/fixed backoff
  - CircuitBreaker — failure threshold + auto-recovery
    (Closed → Open → HalfOpen state machine via signals)
  - with_timeout() — async operation timeout
  - sleep() — WASM-compatible async sleep

Runtime hooks system (oxide-core):
  - HookEvent enum: SignalCreate, SignalRead, SignalWrite,
    EffectRun, EffectComplete, BatchStart, BatchEnd
  - set_hook(fn) / clear_hook() — zero-cost when unused
  - High-resolution timestamps via js_sys::Date::now()

fix: resolve three wrapper generation bugs found via stress testing

1. Scan function parameter/return types for opaque struct references
   Types like sqlite3_blob only appear in function signatures (not struct
   members) and were missing forward declarations, causing UndefVarError
   at module load time.

2. Exclude enum names from forward declarations
   Enum types (e.g., http_method) defined via @enum were getting duplicate
   empty struct forward declarations that shadowed the enum definition.

3. Sanitize union accessor type names and defer emission
   - Union member type names now go through _sanitize_julia_type_name()
     to match actual struct definitions (e.g., __pthread_mutex_s → _pthread_mutex_s)
   - Unknown Ptr{X} inner types fall back to Ptr{Cvoid}
   - Union accessor functions are deferred to after all struct definitions
     to avoid forward reference errors

Validated against: SQLite (269 functions), cJSON (79 functions),
tinyexpr (7 functions), http-parser (15 functions with enums),
Duktape (JS engine), and the existing test suite (81 tests pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add environment diagnostics, package scaffolding, auto MLIR build, hash caching, philosophy docs

- EnvironmentDoctor: check_environment() validates LLVM 21+, Clang, mlir-tblgen,
  CMake, libJLCS with colorful per-OS fix instructions. Auto-runs before build().
- Scaffold: scaffold_package() generates distributable Julia wrapper package

- deps/build.jl: auto-compiles JLCS MLIR dialect on Pkg.add with source hash
  caching. Graceful degradation if toolchain missing (Tier 1 still works).
- Compiler: project-level content hash cache (toml + sources + headers + git HEAD).
  Cached rebuild returns in <1ms. Per-file mtime cache still used for IR files.
- README: Philosophy section explaining source-based approach vs JLLs/BinaryBuilder.
- Version bump: 2.2.1 → 2.3.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Wrapper: generate Base.getproperty accessors for byte-blob structs

Generate property accessors that use DWARF member offsets to provide
named field access on byte-blob structs. For primitive members, uses
unsafe_load with typed pointers. For nested struct members, checks
whether the target is a byte-blob (extract bytes) or normal struct
(unsafe_load typed pointer). Handles embedded-vs-standalone size
mismatches with zero-padding.

Also adds test/realworld_eigen/verify.jl integration test covering
all 5 API functions with struct size and property accessor assertions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Compiler: deferred member flush for correct DWARF offsets; Wrapper: struct dedup and forward-decl fixes

Compiler.jl:
- Add flush_pending_member() that defers adding members to parent structs
  until the next DW_TAG_* boundary, ensuring DW_AT_data_member_location
  is captured before the member dict is copied
- Remove old eager-copy block that produced stale offset=nothing members
- Extend get_type_size() to strip const/volatile/mutable qualifiers and
  handle ptrdiff_t, wchar_t

Wrapper.jl:
- Add best_dwarf_key pre-merge map: when multiple DWARF keys sanitize to
  the same Julia name, prefer the definition with largest byte_size
- Add dwarf_defined_names set to prevent forward declarations from blocking
  real DWARF struct definitions

All 28 core tests pass. Eigen structs now have correct sizes and member
offsets (e.g. ModelData: 48 bytes with offsets 0x0/0x18/0x28).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
